objective: Define and implement repo-level Local CI Gate hardgate wiring so the gate is required where claimed, not merely runnable.
scope_boundary: Plan the enforcement surface before changing workflows, hooks, or ruleset documentation.
Treat CI as authoritative and keep Local CI Gate evidence subordinate to protected-branch checks.
Differentiate manual local invocation, pre-push hook enforcement, GitHub Actions enforcement, and branch-protection or ruleset-required enforcement.
Implement only governance-repo hardgate wiring in this issue; consumer repo rollout remains separate issue-backed work.
Require explicit proof before the closeout claims any enforcement state.
out_of_scope: Installing or refreshing Local CI Gate shims in knocktracker, AIS, LAM, HealthcarePlatform, or ASC-Evaluator.
Changing consumer repo branch protection or local hooks.
Claiming protected-branch enforcement without GitHub ruleset or branch-protection evidence.
Using dry-run output as live enforcement proof.
Editing dirty shared checkouts outside this isolated governance worktree.
research_summary: Issue #275 repaired portable shim behavior, runner invocation evidence, and active execution-scope handling, but intentionally left repo-level hardgate wiring to #277 because workflow and hook files were outside #275 scope. The next slice must add a required invocation path for hldpro-governance and record exactly which enforcement layer is live.

sprints.name: Planning and Enforcement Decision
sprints.goal: Land a trusted plan that defines which hardgate layer issue #277 will implement.
tasks: Record the selected enforcement target for this repo: GitHub Actions Local CI Gate check first, with ruleset requirement evidence handled explicitly.
Declare the implementation write scope for workflow, validation script, runbook, status, closeout, and execution-scope artifacts.
Clarify that pre-push hooks are not the first #277 implementation target unless explicitly added by a later approved slice.
Update backlog and progress mirrors so #275 is closed and #277 is the active Local CI hardgate lane.
acceptance_criteria: The planning PR contains only planning, scope, cross-review, and status artifacts.
The plan states which enforcement layer can be claimed after implementation and which layer remains external or follow-up.
Consumer rollout remains out of scope.

sprints.name: GitHub Actions Gate Wiring
sprints.goal: Add a governance-repo GitHub Actions workflow that runs Local CI Gate as a PR check.
tasks: Add a dedicated workflow for Local CI Gate on pull requests to main and pushes to main.
Run the live hldpro-governance profile, not dry-run mode.
Use full git history (`fetch-depth: 0`) or an explicit changed-files file so the profile's `origin/main...HEAD` resolution is reliable.
Upload or expose local report evidence without committing per-run report artifacts.

Add a workflow contract test that fails if the workflow drifts to dry-run-only, loses PR coverage, uses shallow checkout without explicit changed files, or stops invoking the hldpro-governance profile.
Wire the workflow contract test into an existing independent CI-visible workflow so the Local CI Gate workflow cannot silently remove both the live invocation and its own contract check.
Ensure workflow failure blocks the workflow job when Local CI Gate returns a blocker exit.
acceptance_criteria: A PR touching Local CI Gate surfaces shows a Local CI Gate status check.
The workflow invokes `tools/local-ci-gate/bin/hldpro-local-ci run --profile hldpro-governance` without `--dry-run`.
The workflow uses full checkout history or passes an explicit changed-files file.
The workflow contract test passes locally and in CI.
An independent existing PR check runs the workflow contract test.
The closeout records exact GitHub check names and links.

sprints.name: Required-Status Evidence Boundary
sprints.goal: Record whether the new GitHub Actions gate is required by branch protection or rulesets.
tasks: Inspect current hldpro-governance branch protection and org/repo rulesets for required status checks.

If Local CI Gate can be made required through available GitHub API permissions, update the canonical required-check and ruleset records alongside the exact ruleset/protection change and backup evidence.
If external configuration cannot be changed safely in this slice, document the limitation and leave the workflow check as CI-visible but not protected-branch-required; keep issue #277 open.
Update the runbook taxonomy so future operators do not confuse a visible PR check with a branch-protection-required check.
acceptance_criteria: Closeout states one of: CI-visible workflow check only, or branch-protection/ruleset-required check with evidence.
Any external ruleset change is backed by a captured before/after artifact or API output summary.
Any external ruleset change updates `GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_REQUIRED_CHECK_BASELINE.md`, `GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_RULESET_RECOMMENDATIONS.md`, and the exception register if applicable.
No claim of protected-branch enforcement is made without evidence.
Issue #277 remains open if protected-branch enforcement is not completed.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verified that #275 repaired shim portability, active execution-scope selection, changed-file detection, invocation metadata, and boundary language.
specialist_reviews.summary: No #275 merge blockers remained; #277 is the intentionally separated hardgate-wiring follow-up.

specialist_reviews.focus: Checked that #275 acceptance evidence was sufficient and that CI/hook hardgate claims were not overstated.
specialist_reviews.summary: Accepted #275 evidence with the caveat that dirty forbidden roots kept local scope assertion blocked. Confirmed #277 tracks the remaining hardgate layer.

alternate_model_review.summary: Operator approved moving from #275 remediation to the #277 hardgate planning lane after PR #278 merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: After planning PR merge, issue #277 may add governance-repo Local CI Gate workflow wiring, workflow contract validation, runbook/status updates, and closeout evidence. Consumer repo rollout remains out of scope.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge the planning/scope PR, then implement on branch feature/issue-277-local-ci-hardgate-enforcement.
blocked_on: planning PR merged so implementation scope is trusted from main
independent review of the hardgate enforcement plan

material_deviation_rules: If implementation needs consumer repo changes, stop and create the consumer issue first.
If protected-branch enforcement requires external ruleset changes that cannot be safely verified, document the limitation, keep issue #277 open, and do not claim protected enforcement.
If the Local CI workflow is only visible but not required, call it CI-visible, not branch-protection-required.
If local scope assertion fails because forbidden roots are dirty, record it as locally blocked and do not treat it as passing.
If the workflow must run dry-run only for stability, stop and revise acceptance criteria before merge.
